paper_title: The contrasting effects of thermodynamic and dynamic processes on East Asian summer monsoon precipitation during the Last Glacial Maximum: a data-model comparison

methods.description: This method uses the water vapor budget equation to investigate physical processes associated with the simulated response of East Asian Summer Monsoon (EASM) precipitation to Last Glacial Maximum (LGM) cooling.

steps.description: Neglecting the tendency term in the steady state, column-integrated moisture budget equation can be expressed.
Decompose advection terms into thermodynamic contribution (TC), dynamic contribution (DC), and non-linear (NL) effects.
steps.searchable_summary: decompose advection terms into TC, DC, and NL effects

steps.description: Use moist static energy (MSE) to diagnose large-scale and long-term mean atmospheric vertical motion.
steps.searchable_summary: diagnose atmospheric vertical motion using MSE

methods.description: This method evaluates the consistency of model simulation against proxy data for the LGM EASM region, focusing on annual and seasonal precipitation.

steps.description: Display data-model comparison over China for annual and seasonal precipitation.
steps.searchable_summary: compare model simulation with proxy data for precipitation

steps.description: Analyze the overall agreement and disagreement between model and proxy data.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze agreement and disagreement in data-model comparison
